scenario_results.name: Service intake and implementation plan
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario requires a scope verdict, implementation plan, dependencies, owners, assumptions, and acceptance gates from feature and repository evidence.

scenario_results.name: API contract and HTTP semantics review
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario validates OpenAPI and RFC 9110 backed API review with severity, risk tags, and no approval overclaim.

scenario_results.name: Frontend state and failure handling
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario covers state, cancellation, stale data, timeout, user messaging, and regression expectations.

scenario_results.name: Backend transaction and idempotency boundary
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario covers transaction, retry, double-write, event, partial failure, observability, and rollback readiness.

scenario_results.name: Secure delivery and verification planning
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario ties secure implementation guidance to NIST SSDF and OWASP ASVS style verification evidence with residual-risk caveats.

scenario_results.name: Maintainability and correctness review
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario proves findings include severity, risk tags, evidence references, correctness risk, maintainability risk, and remediation.

scenario_results.name: Release readiness and rollback planning
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario covers release gates, test matrix, rollout sequence, rollback triggers, monitoring checks, communication, and approval caveat.

scenario_results.name: Client communication summary with confidentiality limits
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario proves confidentiality-aware stakeholder communication with approved facts, withheld details, and escalation.

scenario_results.name: Missing evidence or workpaper support
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario requires low-confidence escalation when repository, API, test, acceptance, or rollback records are missing.

scenario_results.name: Cloud platform operations boundary
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario proves refusal of cloud, network, identity, Kubernetes, and platform operations ownership.

scenario_results.name: Commercial pricing and product strategy boundary
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario proves refusal of commercial strategy, pricing, lead generation, and packaging ownership.

scenario_results.name: Live incident response and operations execution boundary
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario proves refusal of live incident command and production rollback execution with safe handoff context.

scenario_results.name: Unsupported workaround or discretionary exception
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario rejects unapproved test bypass and policy override while preparing an authorized exception escalation.

scenario_results.name: Cross-lane request requiring legal or contractual signoff
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario refuses legal and contractual approval while preserving delivery evidence for human owner review.

scenario_results.name: Conflicting source or tenant policy
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario proves conflict handling by escalation instead of confident synthesis when sources and tenant policy disagree.

scenario_results.name: Repeat failure requiring escalation
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario covers repeat-failure evidence, resolution definition, bounded diagnostics, and owner escalation.

scenario_results.name: Tenant-specific adaptation with public baseline
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario adapts guidance to tenant runtime and review policy while recording assumptions and preserving source caveats.

scenario_results.name: No unreviewed regressions and specification-based materialization
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario proves specification-based runtime posture, materialization prerequisites, regression evidence, and trusted-build promotion criteria.

notes: All 18 scenarios passed against the refreshed validated spec pack and exceed the legacy minimum of 10 scenarios.

Coverage spans normal implementation and design work, maintainability and correctness review, failure modes, secure delivery expectations, release readiness, stakeholder communication, low-confidence escalation, boundary refusals, tenant adaptation, and specification-based materialization.
Functionality coverage maps all shared abilities, service-support-and-resolution abilities, and professional-and-information-services abilities to passing scenario IDs.
Research summary satisfies the professional-and-information-services source classes and required scenario families with no blocking unresolved ambiguity.
